briefs.title: Burnaby shelter at 3020 Gilmore must submit Good Neighbour and Operations Plans before opening
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted to block occupancy of a BC Housing shelter at 3020 Gilmore Diversion until BC Housing and the shelter operator submit a Good Neighbour Plan and an Operational Management Plan that satisfy the City.
briefs.action_required: If you operate near 3020 Gilmore, monitor City updates for the shelter's occupancy approval timeline.

briefs.title: Burnaby Plumbing Permit Fees Changed — What You'll Pay Has Shifted
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed Bylaw #14800, which changes the fees charged for plumbing permits. If you plan to do any plumbing work that requires a permit, the cost to get that permit has changed.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's updated fee schedule before applying for any plumbing permit to budget the correct cost.

briefs.title: Burnaby updates its rules for how Council meetings are run
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed an amendment to its Procedure Bylaw — the rulebook that governs how Council meetings are conducted. The change received all three required readings, meaning it is now approved.

briefs.title: 8265–8303 10th Avenue rezoned — land use rules for these properties have officially changed
briefs.summary: City council gave final approval to rezone (officially change the permitted land uses) for several properties on 10th Avenue. Businesses or property owners at 8265–8303 10th Avenue are now subject to new zoning rules.
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease property at 8265–8303 10th Avenue, check the new zoning rules to confirm your business use is still permitted.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby now regulates bear spray sales — business licence rules updated
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed Bylaw #14798, amending the Business Licence Bylaw 2017 to add rules around the sale of bear spray. Businesses that sell bear spray in Burnaby must now comply with these new licence conditions.
briefs.action_required: Check your Burnaby business licence conditions if you sell bear spray and ensure you meet the new requirements.

briefs.title: Burnaby Bylaw Now Regulates Bear Spray — Violations Can Result in Fines
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed Bylaw #14799, adding bear spray to its Bylaw Notice Enforcement rules. This means carrying or using bear spray in ways that break the bylaw can now result in a formal fine notice.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business sells or allows bear spray on-site and review Burnaby's updated bylaw rules.

briefs.title: Buller Avenue Properties Move Closer to Rezoning After Council's First Two Readings
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved the first and second readings of a zoning bylaw amendment (a change to local land-use rules) for two properties at 7305 and 7317 Buller Avenue. The rezoning process is now underway but not yet final.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 7305–7317 Buller Ave, monitor future Council meetings for the public hearing date.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA Renewal Approved — Businesses in the Area Will Continue Paying Levies
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed the bylaw renewing the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a defined zone where local businesses collectively fund shared improvements and promotions. Businesses within the BIA boundary will continue to pay an annual levy (a mandatory fee collected through property taxes).
briefs.action_required: Check if your business is inside the Burnaby North Road BIA boundary and confirm your annual levy amount.

briefs.title: Active transportation education and promotion programmes approved for 2026
briefs.summary: Council has approved a set of education and promotion initiatives for active transportation (walking, cycling, and other non-motorized travel) for 2026, based on a Transportation Committee report from February 11, 2026.

briefs.title: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw text amendment passes first and second reading
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council gave first and second reading to a text amendment (a written change to the rules) to the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965. The amendment is not yet final and must pass a third reading before it becomes law.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council agendas for the third reading and final adoption of Bylaw #14792.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 20-Storey Rental Tower at 5058 Imperial Street Replaces Approved 6-Storey Plan
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has approved moving forward with a rezoning (a change to land-use rules) for a 20-storey mixed-use rental building at 5058 Imperial Street, replacing a previously approved 6-storey plan.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near 5058 Imperial Street, monitor rezoning progress for potential impacts.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Burnaby Lake Village rezoning bylaw advances, allowing taller buildings and updated open space rules
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved a rezoning bylaw (a rule that changes how land can be used or built on) for the Blake Village Master Plan at 6800 Lougheed Highway, allowing updated building heights, massing, and open space requirements aligned with the city's long-term plan.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near 6800 Lougheed Highway, monitor future public hearings for this rezoning.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 49-Storey Mixed-Use Tower at Springer Avenue Advances; City Land Sale Approved in Principle
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved first reading of a rezoning bylaw for a 49-storey residential and retail tower at 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue, and agreed in principle to sell City-owned land to include in the development site.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near Springer Avenue, monitor future Council readings for rezoning approval and any related disruptions.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: On-Street Public Pay Parking Fees Increased by City Council
briefs.summary: City council has approved an increase to on-street public pay parking rates, as outlined in the Financial Management Committee report from February 17, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Update your budget and customer communications to reflect higher on-street parking costs nearby.

briefs.title: City-owned EV charging stations switch to energy-based fees under new bylaw amendments
briefs.summary: City council approved a new pricing model for public EV charging stations it owns — drivers will now pay based on energy used (kilowatt-hours) instead of time parked. Three city bylaws were updated to reflect this change.
briefs.action_required: If your business uses or manages City-owned EV chargers, review the new energy-based fee schedule to update your cost estimates.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: City renews $162,400 portable toilet contract with Jiffy John Rentals
briefs.summary: Council approved a contract renewal with Jiffy John Rentals Ltd. to supply portable toilets at an estimated cost of $162,400 including taxes.

briefs.title: City renews $670,000 security contract with Guardteck for one year
briefs.summary: City council approved a one-year renewal of its security services contract with Guardteck Security Corp, covering officer services at city buildings and special events, valued at an estimated $670,000 including taxes.

briefs.title: Heritage Alteration Permit granted for chimney work at 6490 Deer Lake Avenue, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved a Heritage Alteration Permit (official permission to modify a protected historic building) for chimney changes at the W.J. Mathers House 'Altnadene' at 6490 Deer Lake Avenue.

briefs.title: Up to 84 outdoor events approved at Burnaby venues March–November 2026, with noise bylaw exemptions
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved noise bylaw exemptions for up to 84 outdoor events at Deer Lake, Central Park, and Swangard Stadium between March 15 and November 1, 2026.
briefs.action_required: If you operate near these venues, plan for increased noise and crowds on event days in 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby outdoor event season begins — noise bylaw exemptions take effect
Burnaby outdoor event season ends — noise bylaw exemptions expire

briefs.title: Commercial fleet parking permit renewed at 7000 Lougheed Highway
briefs.summary: Council approved the renewal of a Temporary Use Permit (a short-term permission to use land in a way not normally allowed by zoning) for commercial fleet vehicle parking and rentals at 7000 Lougheed Highway.

briefs.title: Burnaby freezes $63,005 FCM membership dues until city gets Big City Mayors' Caucus seat
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has paused its annual membership payment of $63,005 to the Federation of Canadian Municipalities (FCM) until the city is given a seat on the Big City Mayors' Caucus, a national group that gives large cities a direct voice in federal policy.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves Option B design for new Landmark Burnaby Sign
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the Option B design concept for the Landmark Burnaby Sign, directing staff to move forward with the project as described in the February 24, 2026 report.

briefs.title: Antenna variance permit at 9001 University Crescent sent back for further staff review
briefs.summary: A permit application to allow antenna equipment that exceeds normal size and height limits at 9001 University Crescent has been sent back to city staff for more review before a decision is made.

briefs.title: Construction permits in Lower Westridge may pause during new natural hazard safety review
briefs.summary: City council launched a city-wide Natural Hazard Development Permit program and approved $120,000 to assess land safety risks in Lower Westridge. Council also asked the Province to temporarily stop issuing new building permits in Lower Westridge until the assessment is done.
briefs.action_required: If you plan to build in Lower Westridge, check with the city before applying for a construction permit.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council grants utility access easement over 3860 Sperling Avenue for Recreation Centre project
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved a Statutory Right-of-Way (a legal permission allowing utility companies to access and use a strip of private or public land) over part of 3860 Sperling Avenue to connect utilities to the Burnaby Lake Recreation Centre.

briefs.title: Antenna variance application at 8888 University Drive sent back for further staff review
briefs.summary: A permit application to allow antenna equipment to exceed normal size and height limits at 8888 University Drive has been sent back to city staff for more review before a decision is made.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA renewed for 2026–2031 with new bylaw coming
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a five-year renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Association (BIA — a group that collects levies from local businesses to fund area improvements) and is preparing a new BIA Bylaw for 2026.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business is in the North Road BIA zone — you may owe a new levy starting 2026.

briefs.title: Burnaby Now Regulates Bear Spray Sales — Retailers Must Meet New Licence Rules
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has amended its Business Licence Bylaw and Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to add new rules around the retail sale of bear spray. Stores that sell bear spray in Burnaby must now comply with these updated licensing requirements.
briefs.action_required: Check your Burnaby business licence conditions to confirm you meet the new bear spray sales requirements.

briefs.title: Plumbing Permit Fees Changing — What Construction Businesses Need to Know
briefs.summary: City council approved small changes to plumbing permit fees and directed the City Solicitor to write a new bylaw to put those changes into effect.
briefs.action_required: Check the updated plumbing permit fee schedule before submitting your next permit application.

briefs.title: City will now remove outdated land restrictions from private properties on request
briefs.summary: The City has approved the removal of old restrictive covenants (legal rules tied to a property that limit how it can be used) that it previously placed on private land. Property owners can now ask the City Solicitor to formally remove these outdated restrictions.
briefs.action_required: If your property has a City-imposed restrictive covenant, contact the City Solicitor's office to request its removal.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Retail
